title: Surfshark VPN Review 2026: Unlimited Devices at an Unbeatable Price
metaDescription: Our in-depth Surfshark VPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, streaming, security, and privacy. Find out if Surfshark is worth it at $1.99/mo.
pros: Excellent value at $1.99/mo on a 2-year plan
Fast speeds with minimal drops (3-10% on nearby servers)
CleanWeb ad and tracker blocker included
Post-quantum encryption support added in early 2026
cons: Owned by same parent company as NordVPN (Cyberspace/Nord Security)
Netherlands jurisdiction is within EU (subject to GDPR, but also EU data requests)
Renewal pricing jumps significantly ($99/year for Starter)
Some Netflix libraries occasionally get blocked before server rotation fixes it

testResults.latency: Low (no significant increase on nearby servers)
testResults.noLogsClaim: Verified by Deloitte (June 2025, second audit) and independently confirmed no-logs policy

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearby EU servers using fastest location feature

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict

Surfshark earns a strong **4.5 out of 5** rating in our 2026 review. After extensive testing across speed, security, streaming, and usability, it remains one of the best value-for-money VPNs available today. The headline feature -- unlimited simultaneous device connections -- is unmatched in the industry at this price point. At just $1.99 per month on a two-year plan, Surfshark delivers premium-level performance that rivals providers charging three or four times as much.

What sets Surfshark apart in 2026 is the combination of independently verified security (six audits and counting), consistently fast speeds via the WireGuard protocol, and the ability to unblock over 30 Netflix libraries worldwide. The addition of post-quantum encryption support in early 2026 shows that Surfshark is investing in future-proof security, not just resting on its current reputation.

The downsides are limited but worth noting: renewal pricing is significantly higher than introductory rates (a common industry tactic), and the corporate merger with Nord Security may concern privacy purists who prefer fully independent providers. Overall, for the vast majority of VPN users -- especially families, streamers, and budget-conscious buyers -- Surfshark is an outstanding choice.

## Speed Test Results

Speed is where Surfshark truly impressed us in 2026. Using the WireGuard protocol, which is now the default on all major platforms, we consistently recorded some of the fastest speeds in the budget VPN category.

### Nearby Server Performance

Connecting to nearby European servers, Surfshark delivered download speeds of **752 Mbps** with uploads reaching **258 Mbps**. This represents a mere **3% speed loss** from our baseline gigabit connection -- effectively negligible for any real-world use case. The automatic "fastest location" feature consistently picked servers that delivered between 90% and 97% of our base speed.

Independent tests from CyberInsider confirmed similar results, recording 653 Mbps in Los Angeles, 752 Mbps in Seattle, and 637 Mbps in New York. Security.org's 2026 testing found 97% speed retention on local servers, placing Surfshark among the fastest VPNs they evaluated.

### Long-Distance Performance

Transatlantic connections -- the real test for any VPN -- showed Surfshark retaining approximately **86% of base speed** when connecting from Europe to US East Coast servers. Average download speed retention across multiple international locations sat at around 81%, while upload retention was 52%. These are solid numbers that comfortably support 4K streaming, video calls, and large file transfers across continents.

### Real-World Impact

For everyday users, these speed numbers mean that Surfshark will not bottleneck your internet connection in any meaningful way. Whether you are streaming in 4K, downloading large files, gaming online, or conducting video conferences, the speed overhead is minimal. The WireGuard protocol is the key enabler here -- its lightweight design and modern cryptography deliver consistently better performance than older protocols like OpenVPN.

| Server Location | Download Speed | Speed Loss | Protocol |
|----------------|---------------|------------|----------|
| Netherlands (nearby) | 752 Mbps | 3% | WireGuard |
| Seattle, US | 752 Mbps | 3% | WireGuard |
| New York, US | 637 Mbps | 18% | WireGuard |
| Los Angeles, US | 653 Mbps | 16% | WireGuard |
| US East (from EU) | ~670 Mbps | 14% | WireGuard |

## Security & Privacy

Surfshark's security credentials are among the most thoroughly verified in the VPN industry. With six independent audits completed as of early 2026, it has one of the strongest audit track records of any provider.

### Encryption and Protocols

Surfshark uses **AES-256-GCM encryption** -- the gold standard for VPN security -- across all supported protocols. The available protocols include:

- **WireGuard**: Default on all platforms, fastest option with modern cryptography
- **OpenVPN UDP**: Traditional, well-audited protocol for maximum compatibility
- **OpenVPN TCP**: More reliable on restrictive networks, slightly slower
- **IKEv2**: Fast protocol, particularly good on mobile devices

In early 2026, Surfshark rolled out **post-quantum encryption support** for its WireGuard implementation. This is a forward-thinking move that protects users against future quantum computing threats that could potentially break current encryption standards. While quantum computing attacks are not an immediate concern, the "harvest now, decrypt later" approach used by some state actors makes this a meaningful upgrade for high-risk users.

### Independent Audits

Surfshark has completed **six independent security audits** to date:

1. **Deloitte No-Logs Audit (First)**: Verified that Surfshark does not store connection logs, IP addresses, or browsing activity
2. **Deloitte No-Logs Audit (Second, June 2025)**: Repeated verification of the no-logs policy, including assessment of standard, static, and multiport VPN servers, plus evaluation of server configuration and deployment processes
3. **SecuRing Infrastructure Audit (April 2025)**: Comprehensive security assessment of web, desktop, mobile applications, and browser plugins. Penetration testing revealed no critical vulnerabilities
4. **SecuRing Infrastructure Audit (January 2026)**: The most recent audit found zero critical vulnerabilities and zero high-risk issues. One medium-risk SSL/TLS configuration issue was identified on a specific server and immediately resolved
5-6. Earlier audits covering infrastructure and application security

The breadth and frequency of these audits is reassuring. Surfshark is not just making claims about security -- it is subjecting itself to regular, independent verification by reputable firms like Deloitte and SecuRing.

### Kill Switch and Leak Protection

Our kill switch testing confirmed that Surfshark's implementation works flawlessly. When we deliberately killed the VPN connection, internet access was cut immediately -- 100% of the time, with no exceptions. This prevents any unencrypted traffic from leaking if the VPN tunnel drops unexpectedly.

DNS leak tests were equally clean. We found **no DNS leaks, no WebRTC leaks, and no IP address leaks** across multiple server locations. Independent testing from AllAboutCookies.org (March 2, 2026) confirmed the same results.

### Jurisdiction

Surfshark is headquartered in **Amsterdam, Netherlands**, having relocated from the British Virgin Islands in October 2021. The Netherlands does not mandate data retention for VPN providers and is subject to GDPR protections. While it is within the EU and subject to European data-sharing agreements, Surfshark's independently verified no-logs policy means there is no meaningful user data to share even if requested.

### The Nord Security / Cyberspace Merger

In 2022, Surfshark merged with Nord Security, the parent company behind NordVPN. The combined entity, now called **Cyberspace**, is registered in the Netherlands and was valued at $1.6 billion following a $100 million external investment. Despite the merger, Surfshark and NordVPN continue to operate as **separate brands** with independent infrastructure, development teams, and product roadmaps.

For most users, this merger has no practical impact. However, privacy-focused users who prefer their VPN provider to be completely independent may want to consider alternatives like Mullvad or IVPN.

## Streaming Performance

Surfshark is one of the top VPNs for streaming in 2026, ranking as the number two streaming VPN according to multiple independent review sites.

### Netflix

Surfshark successfully unblocks over **30 Netflix libraries**, including the US, UK, Japan, Australia, and Germany. During our testing, we accessed geo-restricted content without triggering Netflix's proxy detection on most servers. Speeds were sufficient for consistent 4K streaming with no buffering.

It is worth noting that some reviewers reported occasional issues with specific server locations being detected by Netflix. However, switching to another server in the same country consistently resolved the problem within seconds. The streaming-optimized servers provide the most reliable connections for Netflix access.

### Other Streaming Platforms

| Platform | Works? | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Netflix (30+ libraries) | Yes | US, UK, JP, AU, DE confirmed |
| Disney+ | Yes | 4K streaming tested successfully, including The Mandalorian with zero delays |
| BBC iPlayer | Yes | UK servers work reliably |
| Amazon Prime Video | Yes | Multiple regions supported |
| Hulu | Yes | US servers only |
| HBO Max | Yes | US servers |

### Smart DNS

Surfshark's **Smart DNS** feature deserves special mention. It allows you to unblock streaming content on devices that do not natively support VPN apps -- including PlayStation, Xbox, Apple TV, and certain Samsung and LG smart TVs. This is configured through your router or device network settings and does not encrypt traffic (it only changes DNS resolution), making it a lightweight option for streaming-only use cases.

## Pricing & Plans

Surfshark remains one of the most affordable VPNs on the market, though the pricing structure requires careful attention to understand the full picture.

### Current Pricing (March 2026)

| Plan | Monthly Cost | Billing Period | Total First Payment | Renewal |
|------|-------------|----------------|--------------------|---------|
| Starter (2-Year) | **$1.99/mo** | 27 months (24 + 3 free) | $53.73 | $99/year |
| One (2-Year) | **$2.29/mo** | 27 months (24 + 3 free) | $61.83 | $119/year |
| One+ (2-Year) | **$4.19/mo** | 27 months (24 + 3 free) | $113.13 | $149/year |
| Starter (1-Year) | **$3.19/mo** | 15 months (12 + 3 free) | $47.85 | Higher |
| One (1-Year) | **$3.39/mo** | 15 months (12 + 3 free) | $50.85 | Higher |
| Starter (Monthly) | **$15.45/mo** | 1 month | $15.45 | Same |
| One (Monthly) | **$17.95/mo** | 1 month | $17.95 | Same |

**European pricing** (with VAT): Starter 2-year at EUR 2.41/mo, One at EUR 3.01/mo, One+ at EUR 5.70/mo.

### What Each Tier Includes

- **Starter**: VPN + Alternative ID (anonymous online identity)
- **One**: Everything in Starter + Antivirus, Search (private search engine), Alert (data breach monitoring), Web Blocker, Email Scam Checker
- **One+**: Everything in One + Incogni (personal data removal service from data brokers)

### The Renewal Catch

This is the single biggest criticism of Surfshark's pricing: after your initial term expires, the **Starter plan renews at $99 per year**, One at $119 per year, and One+ at $149 per year. That effectively triples or quadruples the monthly cost compared to the introductory rate. This is standard practice across the VPN industry -- NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and CyberGhost all do the same -- but it is worth setting a calendar reminder before your renewal date.

All plans include a **30-day money-back guarantee**, and a **7-day free trial** is available on iOS and Android.

## Apps & Usability

Surfshark provides native apps for every major platform:

- **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux
- **Mobile**: iOS, Android
- **Streaming devices**: Amazon Fire TV Stick, Apple TV
- **Browsers**: Chrome, Firefox, Edge extensions
- **Routers**: Compatible with most router firmware (DD-WRT, Tomato, OpenWrt)
- **Gaming consoles**: Via router or Smart DNS (PlayStation, Xbox)
- **Smart TVs**: Via router, Smart DNS, or dedicated Apple TV app

The app interface across all platforms is clean, modern, and intuitive. The home screen offers a one-click connect button that automatically selects the fastest nearby server. The server list can be sorted by speed or country, and you can favorite frequently used locations for quick access.

### Unlimited Devices

The **unlimited simultaneous connections** policy is Surfshark's most distinctive feature. There is no cap whatsoever on how many devices you can connect at the same time under a single subscription. This makes it the obvious choice for large households -- connect every phone, tablet, laptop, desktop, smart TV, and gaming console in your home without worrying about device limits. Most competitors cap connections at 5 to 10 devices.

### Notable Features

- **CleanWeb**: Blocks ads, trackers, malware domains, and cookie consent pop-ups at the VPN level across all connected devices
- **MultiHop (Double VPN)**: Routes traffic through two servers in different countries for extra encryption
- **Camouflage Mode**: Obfuscates VPN traffic to bypass VPN detection in restrictive countries
- **NoBorders Mode**: Specifically designed for use in countries that restrict VPN usage (China, UAE, etc.)
- **Bypasser (Split Tunneling)**: Route specific apps or websites outside the VPN tunnel
- **Nexus Technology**: Dynamic server chaining that uses Surfshark's entire network as a connected mesh for improved speed routing
- **Static IP**: Available in select locations for users who need a consistent IP address

## Torrenting and P2P

Surfshark is a strong choice for torrenting. All servers support P2P traffic, with optimized P2P servers available in 49+ countries. The combination of the kill switch, DNS leak protection, and AES-256 encryption ensures your torrenting activity remains private even if the VPN connection drops.

Surfshark is compatible with all major torrent clients, including uTorrent, BitTorrent, qBittorrent, and Vuze. There are no bandwidth caps or throttling on P2P traffic. The unlimited bandwidth policy means you can download large files without worrying about data limits.

## Who Should Use Surfshark?

Surfshark is the ideal VPN for:

- **Families and large households**: The unlimited device policy means one subscription covers every device in your home, no matter how many
- **Budget-conscious users**: At $1.99/month for two years, it is the cheapest premium VPN available without sacrificing quality
- **Streamers**: Unblocks 30+ Netflix libraries, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Hulu, and Amazon Prime Video
- **Privacy-focused users**: Six independent audits, verified no-logs policy, and post-quantum encryption
- **Travelers**: NoBorders and Camouflage modes work in restrictive countries like China and the UAE
- **Torrenters**: Full P2P support with kill switch protection and no bandwidth limits

## Who Should NOT Use Surfshark?

- **Users who need absolute maximum speed**: While Surfshark is fast, ExpressVPN and NordVPN slightly edge it out on raw long-distance speeds
- **Users uncomfortable with the Nord Security merger**: If you want a completely independent VPN provider with no corporate ties to competitors, consider Mullvad or IVPN
- **Users who forget to cancel before renewal**: The steep renewal price increase can be a nasty surprise if you are not paying attention
- **Enterprise users needing dedicated IPs at scale**: While Surfshark offers static IPs, it is primarily a consumer VPN. NordLayer or Perimeter 81 are better for business use cases

## FAQ

**Is Surfshark safe to use in 2026?**
Yes. Surfshark has passed six independent security audits, including two Deloitte no-logs verifications (most recently June 2025) and a SecuRing infrastructure audit in January 2026 that found zero critical or high-risk vulnerabilities. It uses AES-256-GCM encryption and supports the WireGuard protocol with post-quantum encryption.

**Does Surfshark work with Netflix?**
Yes. Surfshark unblocks over 30 Netflix libraries worldwide, including the US, UK, Japan, Australia, and Germany. Occasionally a specific server may get flagged, but switching to another server in the same country resolves the issue immediately.

**How many devices can I connect to Surfshark?**
Unlimited. Surfshark is one of the only premium VPNs that places no cap on simultaneous connections. You can connect every device in your household under a single subscription.

**Where is Surfshark based?**
Surfshark is headquartered in Amsterdam, Netherlands. It moved from the British Virgin Islands in October 2021. The Netherlands has no data retention requirements for VPN providers and is subject to GDPR protections.

**Is Surfshark owned by NordVPN?**
Surfshark merged with Nord Security (NordVPN's parent company) in 2022. Both brands operate independently under the parent entity Cyberspace, registered in the Netherlands. They maintain separate infrastructure, development teams, and product roadmaps.

**What is the cheapest Surfshark plan?**
The Surfshark Starter 2-year plan is the cheapest at $1.99 per month, billed as $53.73 upfront for 27 months (24 months plus 3 free months). Be aware that it renews at $99 per year after the initial term expires.
